title: QuEra and Roadrunner to Bring Quantum Platform to New Mexico | Roadrunner Venture Studios
why:
- QuEra Computing and Roadrunner Venture Studios announced a $4M strategic partnership to build a quantum testbed at the Roadrunner Quantum Lab in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
- The new facility will establish a physical presence for QuEra in New Mexico, including hiring full-time employees and expanding access to its quantum computing systems for resident companies.
- The testbed will provide infrastructure for R&D across multiple domains, including a Photonics and Optics Testing Center for neutral atom quantum computing research and a Classical Compute User-Access Facility for secure, remote, hybrid quantum-classical workloads.
- This initiative supports workforce training, startup formation, and increased federal and private capital, aiming to build a durable advanced technology economy in New Mexico.
- The partnership is part of New Mexico's $300 million effort to build a globally competitive quantum economy, attracting leading quantum companies to the state's innovation ecosystem.
- The facilities will initially support academic and national lab partners before expanding access to industry collaborators and quantum startups.
